market_context: US Dollar Index futures (DXH26) hit a 3.5-month high at 100.295, up 3.44% over the past month amid Thursday's stock slump boosting dollar demand. Pre-market futures show broad declines of about 0.5%, with Russell weakest (down 33 handles), Dow/NASDAQ/S&P slightly stronger but still negative; oil spiked nearly 8% overnight to 96 before pulling back to 93, spooking markets. Gold down 0.63%, silver up 1.75%, with high volatility in energy.

predictions.reasoning: Pre-market S&P futures down ~0.5% following Thursday's slump, with Russell even weaker at -33 handles and broad risk-off sentiment. Oil's overnight 8% spike to 96 (now 93) adds inflation fears, pressuring equities. Dollar index at 3.5-month high of 100.295 supports USD strength, typically bearish for stocks.

NASDAQ futures down 0.5% in pre-market, lagging slightly behind Dow/S&P amid broad declines. High oil volatility (up $5.60 but off highs) and strong dollar (DXY +0.39% to 100.130) weigh on tech-heavy QQQ. Continued risk-off from prior session's weakness points to modest further pullback.

Oil futures spiked nearly 8% overnight to 96 before retreating to 93, with high IV rank and steep backwardation signaling supply disruption pricing. Silver up 1.75% adds commodity strength; despite pullback, elevated volatility warrants forward trade bias higher into session.

Gold futures (GCJ26) down 0.63% at 5,093.4 amid dollar strength to 100.295 high. Pre-market equity weakness boosts liquidity demand for USD over safe-haven gold. Limited upside catalysts with oil dominating commodity moves.
